name: Conca de Barberà Cistercian Route MTB Loop
name_original: La Conca de Barberà – vuelta desde Barberà de la Conca por Sarral y Montblanc
description: This moderate 23.6-mile mountain bike route through La Conca de Barberà offers vineyards, historical landmarks, and scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Barberà De La Conca offers a network of trails through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, vineyards, and agricultural fields. The region features a mix of open terrain and forested sections, providing varied riding experiences. Elevations are generally moderate, with some routes including more significant climbs. This area is suitable for exploring the rural beauty of Catalonia on two wheels. Best mountain bike trails around Barberà De La Conca The most popular mountain bike trail is Conca de Barberà Cistercian Route MTB Loop, a 23.6 miles (37.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete, passing through historical Cistercian sites. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Selmella Castle – Cabra del Camp loop from Cabra del Camp, a difficult 22.0 miles (35.3 km) path offering panoramic views of the surrounding countryside and a historic castle. Local mountain bikers also love the Old Bridge of Montblanc – Castillo de Barberà loop from Barberà de la Conca, a 21.9 miles (35.2 km) trail leading through historical towns and past ancient structures, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Barberà De La Conca is defined by rolling hills, vineyards, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more advanced mountain bike routes. The routes in Barberà De La Conca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Barberà De La Conca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Barberà De La Conca?
A: There are over 200 mountain bike routes around Barberà De La Conca, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Barberà De La Conca?
A: Yes, Barberà De La Conca offers 25 easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature rolling hills and less technical terrain.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of mountain bike trails in this region?
A: The mountain bike trails in Barberà De La Conca vary significantly in length and duration. You'll find routes ranging from shorter rides of around 10-15 km to longer excursions exceeding 40 km. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961054804/conca-de-barbera-cistercian-route-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conca de Barberà Cistercian Route MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 37.9 km and takes about 1 hour 54 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31552234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Montblanc – Montblanc City Walls loop from Barberà de la Conca&lt;/a&gt; is 46.1 km and can take over 4 hours.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are 49 difficult routes available. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33857941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selmella Castle – Cabra del Camp loop from Cabra del Camp&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35.3 km long and features significant elevation changes, offering panoramic views and a demanding ride.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Barberà De La Conca?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, with pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, though some trails might be affected by rain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Barberà De La Conca?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Barberà De La Conca are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961054804/conca-de-barbera-cistercian-route-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conca de Barberà Cistercian Route MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19836952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Montblanc – Castillo de Barberà loop from Barberà de la Conca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Barberà De La Conca offer diverse scenery, including rolling hills, expansive vineyards, and agricultural fields. Many routes provide panoramic views of the surrounding countryside, often featuring historical landmarks and charming rural landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Barberà De La Conca?
A: The komoot community highly rates the mountain biking experience in Barberà De La Conca, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the blend of open spaces and forested sections, and the opportunity to explore historical sites and rural beauty on two wheels.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Many routes pass by or near historical sites. For instance, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1726823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Montblanc&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4272355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Josep de Montblanc&lt;/a&gt;, or even ancient structures like the Castillo de Barberà. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961054804/conca-de-barbera-cistercian-route-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conca de Barberà Cistercian Route MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights historical Cistercian sites.

Q: Are there any natural attractions like waterfalls or springs to see?
A: Yes, the area around Barberà De La Conca features several natural highlights. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2216720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vall Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3987272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt of the Barranc de la Vall&lt;/a&gt;, as well as springs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4748206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deport Spring&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into your rides.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of historical sites and natural beauty?
A: Many routes in Barberà De La Conca combine both. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19836952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Montblanc – Castillo de Barberà loop from Barberà de la Conca&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through historical towns and past ancient structures while traversing scenic landscapes.
tips: Medieval building converted into a farmhouse. The castle still retains some old elements, although the multiple transformations it has undergone are also visible.
Old medieval bridge that crosses the Francolí River on its way through Montblanc.
Majestic Gothic-style church, located at the highest point of Montblanc. The temple also known as the Cathedral of the Mountain.
Small hermitage in Gothic style with a rectangular plan located in Montornès. After being abandoned, a recovery campaign has managed to restore the church.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the La Conca de Barberà mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a moderate mountain bike route covering approximately 37.9 kilometers with about 500 meters of elevation gain. While the raw cycling time is around 1 hour 54 minutes, most riders with good fitness should plan for a more realistic duration of 3-4 hours, allowing for stops to enjoy the scenery and points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate and requires a good level of physical fitness. The terrain is varied, typical of the Conca de Barberà region, featuring rides through vineyards and cultivated fields, as well as sections that may include smoother paths and potentially more rugged parts suitable for mountain biking in the inland mountains.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Barberà de la Conca. Specific parking details are not provided, but you can typically find parking within the village of Barberà de la Conca. It's a circular route, so you'll return to your starting point.

faqs.question: What historical and cultural landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich cultural experience. You'll pass by significant landmarks such as Barberà Castle, the Old Bridge of Montblanc, and the Santa Maria Church (Montblanc). You'll also be near the Hermitage of Santa Anna de Montornès. The region itself is part of the Cistercian Route and is known for its medieval towns like Montblanc.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and picturesque scenery. The route takes you through expansive vineyards characteristic of the DO Conca de Barberà wine region, cultivated fields, and offers views of traditional villages. From elevated points, you might catch magnificent views of the Conca de Barberà and surrounding mountains.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the region. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages, cultivated areas, or near livestock, and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, and the natural scenery, especially the vineyards, is particularly beautiful. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be chilly.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camí de Sant Jaume de Tarragona a Lleida - Etapa 4, El Camí IT6TR5 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, GR-175 BTT, and the Ruta del Císter.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through villages like Barberà de la Conca, Sarral, and Montblanc, where you can find various amenities. Montblanc, in particular, is a larger medieval town likely offering cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options for visitors.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a charged phone. A GPS device or the komoot app with the route downloaded is also highly recommended for navigation.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Santa Anna de Montornès
